scope: Port config-driven tool enablement and per-tool default approval policy into AgenC's tool registry and approval router

rows.source: AppToolConfig.enabled plus MCP enabled_tools/disabled_tools filter semantics
requiredBehaviors: boolean tool entries remain accepted as enable/disable shorthand
object tool entries can disable a specific tool with enabled=false
enabled_tools acts as an allowlist and disabled_tools wins over that allowlist
disabled tools are absent from registry.tools, provider-visible tool schemas, and dispatch lookup
edgeCases: dotted tool names represented as nested TOML tables
legacy web_search shorthand aliases WebSearch and web_search
dynamic/MCP/model-facing tools share the same filter path
commands: npm run test --workspace=@tetsuo-ai/runtime -- src/tools/config.test.ts src/tool-registry.test.ts src/config/config.test.ts

requiredBehaviors: default_permission_mode carries the existing config approval literals onto registered tools
approval_mode compatibility values map approve to never and prompt to untrusted
the orchestrator uses a tool's default permission mode for initial approval classification
sandbox-denial escalation consults the same effective policy
edgeCases: session policy requires approval but tool default skips
session policy skips approval but tool default requires approval
auto approval_mode leaves the session policy unchanged
commands: npm run test --workspace=@tetsuo-ai/runtime -- src/tools/config.test.ts src/tools/orchestrator.test.ts src/tool-registry.test.ts
